message:
Running command: MCU_DIR="/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu"
WRAPPER_V="$MCU_DIR/verilog/rtl/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper.v"

# The 102 missing ports are all in /tmp/wrapper_ports.txt but NOT in /tmp/def_pins.txt
# Strategy: use python to remove these from the port list and add as internal wires

cat > /tmp/fix_wrapper_ports.py << 'PYEOF'
import re

missing_ports_file = "/tmp/missing_ports.txt"
wrapper_file = "/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/verilog/rtl/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper.v"

# Load missing ports
with open("/dev/stdin") as f:
    missing_ports = set(f.read().strip().split('\n'))

print(f"Missing ports to remove: {len(missing_ports)}")

with open(wrapper_file) as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

# Find lines that declare these missing ports in the module header
# and lines that are just comma (which follow a removed port line)
# Identify module header range (from "module" to ");\n")
in_header = False
header_start = -1
header_end = -1
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if re.match(r'^module\s+mspm33c321a', line):
        in_header = True
        header_start = i
    if in_header and re.match(r'^\);', line):
        header_end = i
        break

print(f"Module header: lines {header_start+1} to {header_end+1}")

# Collect lines to remove from header (lines containing missing port names as inout declarations)
lines_to_remove = set()
for i in range(header_start, header_end+1):
    line = lines[i]
    stripped = line.strip().rstrip(',')
    # Check if this is an inout declaration of a missing port
    m = re.match(r'\s*inout\s+(\w+)', line)
    if m and m.group(1) in missing_ports:
        lines_to_remove.add(i)

print(f"Lines to remove from header: {len(lines_to_remove)}")

# Build new lines
new_lines = []
internal_wires = []
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if i in lines_to_remove:
        # Extract port name for internal wire
        m = re.match(r'\s*inout\s+(\w+)', line)
        if m:
            internal_wires.append(f"wire {m.group(1)};\n")
        # Skip this port declaration
        continue
    # If the previous line was removed and this is a bare comma-only line, might need to clean up
    new_lines.append(line)

# Now we need to add the internal wires after the ");" of the module header
# Find header_end in new_lines (line count changes)
# Insert after the ");" line that ends the port list
new_end_idx = -1
for i, line in enumerate(new_lines):
    if re.match(r'^\);', line) and new_end_idx == -1:
        new_end_idx = i
        break

if new_end_idx >= 0:
    # Insert wire declarations after ");", before first assign/instance
    wire_block = ["\n// Internal analog mux bus signals (not digital wrapper pins)\n"] + internal_wires + ["\n"]
    new_lines = new_lines[:new_end_idx+1] + wire_block + new_lines[new_end_idx+1:]

with open(wrapper_file + ".patched", 'w') as f:
    f.writelines(new_lines)

print(f"Original lines: {len(lines)}")
print(f"New lines: {len(new_lines)}")
print(f"Internal wires added: {len(internal_wires)}")
PYEOF

# Generate the missing ports list (comm output)
comm -23 /tmp/wrapper_ports.txt /tmp/def_pins.txt > /tmp/missing_ports.txt
echo "Missing ports file:"
wc -l /tmp/missing_ports.txt
cat /tmp/missing_ports.txt | head -5

echo ""
echo "Running port fix script..."
python3 /tmp/fix_wrapper_ports.py < /tmp/missing_ports.txt

echo ""
echo "Checking patched file..."
wc -l "$WRAPPER_V.patched"
grep "amuxbus_a," "$WRAPPER_V.patched" | head -3
grep "^wire clk_amuxbus_a" "$WRAPPER_V.patched"
